item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents visibly oppose the statement from their opening turns through their final turns. Agent A starts with individual rights as the starting point and ends rejecting automatic social priority while allowing narrow public-interest limits. Agent B likewise rejects automatic social priority throughout, using an American civil-liberties frame in Indonesian. There is no visible flip or directional drift; both acknowledge public interests such as safety, health, and order while keeping individual rights primary.
